summary: Iter 10 — Admin user management + marketing broadcast feature. Backend 16/16 pytest passing (test_iter10_admin_users_broadcast.py). Frontend smoke E2E executed by main agent: search filter, single-user selection, broadcast modal, and POST /admin/users/broadcast with target=selected returns sent=1 → modal renders '✓ 1 emails enviados'. Regression: iter9 12/12 still green (28/28 total).
applied_fixes_post_test: server.py admin_delete_user: swapped guard order — self-DELETE now returns 'No puedes eliminarte a ti mismo' (was masked by admin guard). 28/28 still passing.
deferred_review_comments: user_ids ignored when target!='selected' — kept silent for now (low risk).
_make_unsubscribe_token has no exp — RGPD-acceptable; revisit if abuse observed.
Broadcast 220ms/user sleep: for target=all on >500 users the request may time out. Future: convert to background task with job-id polling.
GET /admin/users does N+1 enrollment counts — fine at current scale (~80 users); switch to $lookup if it grows.
UserBroadcastIn: max_length on subject/body could move to Pydantic Field for OpenAPI.
/admin/users response includes raw user docs — consider explicit projection if PII fields multiply.
test_credentials: Admin: benitezl@go.ugr.es. JWT minted via create_session_jwt with seeded admin id (admin-<md5(email)[:8]>).
seed_data_creation: Per-test users prefixed test_iter10_*@example.com, auto-cleaned. Founder seat snapshot restored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 10 added GET /admin/users, DELETE /admin/users/{id} (cascade + founder-seat restore), POST /admin/users/broadcast (target=all|enrolled|not_enrolled|selected with marketing_consent + admin filtering and 220ms Resend throttle), and public GET /api/unsubscribe?token=... (RGPD). Frontend has UsersControl + BroadcastModal in Admin.jsx with full data-testid coverage. Resend rate limit: 5/s — keep broadcast tests bounded to target='selected' with ≤2 controlled users.
rca of the issue: No bugs. One small spec-compliance fix applied: self-delete guard order swapped so the 'self' message wins over the 'admin' message.
